quoteLast: when he drew up at the curb in front of the side entrance of the hotel--his watch, set by guesswork, had been a little slow, and he had corrected it at the club. He was replacing the watch in his pocket as he sauntered around the corner, and passed in through the main entrance to the big lobby.
title: The Adventures of Jimmie Dale
author: Packard, Frank L. (Frank Lucius), 1877-1942

quoteLast: ,” said he, pulling out a watch with a nickel case, “and, say put out the light. It would be funny to have two silhouettes of bonze heads on the shoji. The fox is too ready to suspect.”
title: Botchan (Master Darling)
author: Natsume, Soseki, 1867-1916; Mori, Yasotaro, 1882-1959 [Translator]

quoteFirst: The floor was covered with linoleum of black and white squares, and the woodwork was of mahogany and white pine. A brass ship’s clock pointed to
quoteLast: , and two brass lamps promised to afford plenty of light.
title: Four Afloat: Being the Adventures of the Big Four on the Water
author: Barbour, Ralph Henry, 1870-1944

quoteFirst: I was engaged to give a lecture at the City Hall, Glasgow, which was to commence at eight o'clock. The night train to London left at
quoteLast: , so there was not much time to spare. By taking my ticket in the afternoon, leaving my portmanteau in the cloak-room, engaging an intelligent porter to take it out and have it ready for me, and benefiting by the kindness of my host, Wm. Holms, Esq., M.P.
title: Coaching, with Anecdotes of the Road
author: Lennox, William Pitt, Lord, 1799-1881
